text.content:
Shadowrun 2nd Edition uses the Priority system. Work top to bottom — the sheet checks your math as you go.

1 · Concept

Decide who your runner is: a street samurai, mage, decker, rigger, adept, face… This drives every choice below.

2 · Priorities (Bio tab)

On the Bio tab, assign a grade A–E to each of the five categories — Attributes, Skills, Resources, Magic, Race. In Standard mode each letter is used exactly once; the sheet warns you if you duplicate or skip one. See the Priority Table page for what each grade grants.

3 · Race (Bio tab)

Drag your metatype from the SR2E Metatypes compendium onto the sheet. Racial attribute modifiers and maximums apply automatically. (Only Race priority A allows a metahuman; B–E are human.)

4 · Attributes (Attributes tab)

Spend your Attribute Points across the six attributes (Body, Quickness, Strength, Charisma, Intelligence, Willpower). Each starts at 1; no attribute may exceed your racial maximum. Reaction, Essence and Magic are calculated for you — don't buy them. Watch the Attribute Points row in the budget panel (bottom of the Bio tab).

5 · Skills (Skills tab)

Spend Skill Points on Active skills (max rating 6 at creation). Add Knowledge and Language skills lower down — your native language is free at Intelligence + 2. Special skills need GM approval.

6 · Magic (Magic tab)

If your Magic priority is A–C, set your magician type. Spellcasters buy spells by spending Force Points (no spell over Force 6). Physical adepts spend Power Points equal to their Magic rating on adept powers. Mundane? Skip this tab.

7 · Resources (Gear tab)

Tick “Creation in progress” on the Bio tab first — that makes purchases charge list price (no street markup). Then drag gear, cyberware, weapons and a lifestyle onto the sheet; the Resources (¥) budget row tracks your spend. Starting gear can't have a rating over 6, and Essence can't drop below 0.

8 · Finishing touches

You start with two free contacts, a Karma Pool of 1, and must buy a lifestyle (no lifestyle = living on the street). Fill in a name and a few lines of background on the Bio tab.

9 · Lock it in

When every budget row is at or under its allotment, untick “Creation in progress.” From now on purchases pay street price. You're ready to run.

Assign one grade A–E to each column. (SR2E p.44–45.)

GradeAttributesSkillsResourcesMagicRace
A30401,000,000¥ / 50 ForceFull MagicianAny metahuman
B2430400,000¥ / 35 ForceAspected / AdeptHuman
C202490,000¥ / 25 ForceAdeptHuman
D17205,000¥ / 15 Force—Human
E1517500¥ / 5 Force—Human

Force Points (the second number under Resources) go to spellcasters only, for buying spells and bonding foci.

Sum-to-10 variant (optional): if the GM enables it on the Bio tab, grades score A=4, B=3, C=2, D=1, E=0 and must total 10 — duplicates allowed.

Where things live on the sheet

  • Bio tab — priorities, budget panel, “Creation in progress,” Karma ledger, background.
  • Attributes tab — the six attributes, plus auto-calculated Reaction, Initiative, Combat/Magic pools, Condition Monitors and Movement.
  • Skills tab — Active, Build/Repair, Knowledge, Language and Special skills. Click a skill name to roll it; click a (Concentration) or [Specialization] tag to roll that instead.
  • Combat tab — weapons and armour. Every character has a default Unarmed Strike.
  • Magic tab — tradition, spells, foci, spirits, astral state.
  • Matrix tab — cyberdeck and decking.
  • Gear / Vehicles / Contacts — everything you own and everyone you know.

Rolling the dice

SR2 is a Success Test system: roll a pool of d6s against a Target Number, and every die that meets or beats the TN is one success. A 6 explodes (roll again and add — the Rule of Six). Click any skill, attribute or weapon on the sheet to open the roll dialog, where you can add Combat Pool or Karma Pool dice before rolling.

Karma Pool (starts at 1) can be spent to buy an extra success or re-roll failures — use it when a roll really matters.
